seo.title: Sheep tails - glossy brown - Marinda Kook

html:
I know sheep tails aren't something you can just kick out from behind every bush, but these days I find them fairly easily at Checkers here in our town. Otherwise ask your butcher to put some together for you. It's a treat that can work as a main course or just something to nibble on before the main meal comes. These days this once-humble cut has become a sought-after delicacy. I cooked mine in the oven, but it can also be grilled on the braai rack.

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 30 sheep tails
  • 3 whole cloves
  • salt, pepper and coriander to taste
  • 250 ml tomato sauce
  • 100 ml chutney
  • 100 ml Worcester sauce
  • 250 ml white wine
  • 1 onion in rings

NOW:

Slice the onion into rings in an ovenproof dish. Pack the sheep tails on top and season with salt, pepper and coriander. Toss in your 3 whole cloves.

Mix the rest of the ingredients.

Pour over the meat.

Into the oven at 180 degrees Celsius for 45 min, covered. Then uncover and roast for another 90 minutes, uncovered. Turn the tails from time to time.

 

This grand indulgence is finger-lickin' good.
